summary: Topstep scored 83/100 in our 2026 testing, placing 3rd on our list of the 10 best futures prop firms. Topstep has one evaluation step — the Trading Combine — after which traders move to an Express Funded Account where payouts begin. A Live Funded Account is available as a later upgrade. Topstep now offers Standard and No Activation Fee paths for the Trading Combine. For traders who joined on or after 12 January 2026, payouts are subject to a 90/10 split. Payout requests can be made in as little as 3 days, with Express Funded offering Standard and Consistency payout paths. TopstepX is the main current platform.
pros: Up to 90% profit split for new traders
Payouts available in as little as 3 days
No time limits on Trading Combine
Standard and No Activation Fee paths available
cons: 3.6/5 Trustpilot score lower than newer competitors
Additional data fees apply for extra market data feeds
After first payout, Maximum Loss Limit set to $0
bestFor: Futures traders who want an established firm with flexible evaluation paths, structured risk rules, and the option to choose between Standard and No Activation Fee models
notIdealFor: Forex or CFD traders — Topstep is futures-only. Also not ideal for traders who rely on automated trading or need overnight holding

verdictBlocks.heading: Final Verdict on Topstep
verdictBlocks.body:
Topstep is a well-established futures prop firm with one evaluation step — the Trading Combine — followed by an Express Funded Account where payouts begin. A Live Funded Account is available as a later upgrade, not as part of the challenge steps.

Topstep now offers two Trading Combine paths: Standard (lower monthly fee + $149 activation fee when funded) and No Activation Fee (higher monthly fee, no activation fee). For traders who joined on or after 12 January 2026, payouts are subject to a 90/10 split, replacing the older 100% of first $10K policy for new sign-ups.

Payout requests can be made in as little as 3 days, with Express Funded offering both Standard and Consistency payout paths. TopstepX is the main current platform, offering the lowest trading costs. The Trading Combine has no time limits, and the consistency target promotes disciplined trading.

While data fees and platform costs can add up in later stages, Topstep's structured evaluation, flexible paths, and large community make it a solid choice for futures traders.

faq.q: What is the best futures prop firm?
faq.a: FXIFY Futures is the best futures prop firm based on our firsthand testing, scoring 88/100. FundedNext Futures scored 84/100 and Topstep scored 83/100, placing second and third. We reviewed over a dozen futures prop firms, comparing key factors like payout structure, platform access, challenge flexibility, and overall value.

faq.q: What are the requirements to get funded by Topstep?
faq.a: To get funded by Topstep, traders must pass the Trading Combine, meeting profit targets and staying within risk limits. For a $50K account, the profit target is $3,000, the daily loss limit is $1,000, and the maximum loss limit is $2,000. Traders must also demonstrate consistency, ensuring no more than 50% of profits come from one day. After passing the Combine, traders move to an Express Funded Account where payouts begin.

faq.q: What are the requirements to pass the Topstep Trading Combine?
faq.a: To pass the Topstep Trading Combine, traders must meet specific profit targets while staying within risk limits. For a $50K account, the profit target is $3,000, while for a $100K account, it's $6,000. Traders must also avoid exceeding the maximum loss limit (e.g., $2,000 for a $50K account) and daily loss limit (e.g., $1,000 for a $50K account). Additionally, no more than 50% of profits can come from one trading day.

faq.q: How much do Topstep traders make?
faq.a: For traders who joined on or after 12 January 2026, Topstep payouts are subject to a 90/10 split (trader keeps 90%). The older 100% of the first $10,000 policy only applies to traders who signed up before that date. Earnings depend on account size, trading performance, and risk management.

faq.q: Is Topstep a proprietary trading firm?
faq.a: Yes, Topstep is a proprietary trading firm that evaluates traders through the Trading Combine challenge and funds successful candidates via Express Funded Accounts. Topstep specialises in futures markets, covering CME Group products across forex, equity indices, energy, metals, and agriculture.
